**************************************************************************** Feb 3, 2014 CONTENTS Synopsys on Instructional computers Synopsys Licenses for EECS Synopsys Documentation and on-line Help Synopsys buys Synplicity Sentau

and on-line Help Synopsys buys Synplicity Sentaurus (SWB): Tecplot replaced by Svisual Release Notes Synopsys on Instructional computers ----------------------------------- Synopsys (http://www.synopsys.com) is a software simulation and synt

ww.synopsys.com) is a software simulation and synthesis package. It is installed for Instructional users on Solaris and Linux. Some Synopsys tools run only on RedHat/Centos Linux or on Solaris. Newer tools run only on Redhat/Centos Linux.

Newer tools run only on Redhat/Centos Linux. Here are some servers: Linux servers and workstations: hpse-9.eecs.berkeley.edu (64-bit Centos Linux) hpse-10.eecs.berkeley.edu (64-bit Centos Linux) hpse-11.eecs.berkeley.edu (64-bit Centos Li

inux) hpse-11.eecs.berkeley.edu (64-bit Centos Linux) hpse-12.eecs.berkeley.edu (64-bit Centos Linux) hpse-13.eecs.berkeley.edu (64-bit Centos Linux) hpse-14.eecs.berkeley.edu (64-bit Centos Linux) hpse-15.eecs.berkeley.edu (64-bit Centos Linux)

) hpse-15.eecs.berkeley.edu (64-bit Centos Linux) t7400-1.eecs .. t7400-12.eecs (64-bit Centos, 125 Cory) c125-1.eecs .. c125-21.eecs (64-bit Centos, 125 Cory) Solaris SPARC servers (Synopsys has not updated some tools since 2009): c199.eecs

s not updated some tools since 2009): c199.eecs (Solaris) helo.eecs (Solaris) pulsar.eecs (Solaris) quasar.eecs (Solaris) See http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.html#labs for current lists of all of our computers. Students in EECS

ts of all of our computers. Students in EECS classes can obtain Instructional UNIX accounts; please see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.html. A user can login to these servers from a desktop computer that has XWindows enabled so

a desktop computer that has XWindows enabled so the the graphical output will be displayed on that screen. Users need to have an XWindows manager running on their local workstation. On the SunRays in 199 Cory, set the window manager to CDE or

ays in 199 Cory, set the window manager to CDE or Gnome, not to "Java Desktop" (that is done before logging in by selecting the window manager from the "Options" menu on the login screen). For more information, please see http://inst.eecs.ber

ore information, please see http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/connecting.html#xwindows . The most commonly used tools can be run via our startup scripts, such as /share/b/bin/swb (Sentaurus Workbench; start here) /share/b/bin/svisual (Sentaurus V

ch; start here) /share/b/bin/svisual (Sentaurus Visual; replaced TecPlot in Oct 2013) /share/b/bin/cx (Custom Explorer; replaced Waveview in Oct 2013) /share/b/bin/starXtract (StarRC Ultra; replaced StarRC in Oct 2013) /share/b/bin/layout /shar

ed StarRC in Oct 2013) /share/b/bin/layout /share/b/bin/medici /share/b/bin/sdevice /share/b/bin/sprocess /share/b/bin/tdevice /share/b/bin/tprocess /share/b/bin/tsuprem4 /share/b/bin/tv /share/b/bin/twb The Synopsys tools are in /share/i

re/b/bin/twb The Synopsys tools are in /share/instsww/synopsys. To run a specific command that is not listed above, for example: % source /share/instsww/synopsys/setup.csh % mc -tech lsi_10k OR % mc -tech class Note: Th

OR % mc -tech class Note: The error message "Tcl_AppInit failed" probably means you aren't running an XWindow manager on your local desktop. Please report questions or problems to inst@eecs.berkleley.edu. Synopsys Licenses

ems to inst@eecs.berkleley.edu. Synopsys Licenses for EECS -------------------------- The Synopsys "University Bundle" licenses (Synopsys, HSPICE, Sentaurus TCAD) have been purchased by EECS Instruction. The EECS Device Group also uses th

nstruction. The EECS Device Group also uses these licenses. New licenses were installed in Oct 2013 (see 'Tecplot replaced by Svisual' below). EECS Instruction (inst@eecs) supports the installation and usage of Synopsys tools for cl

installation and usage of Synopsys tools for classes on the Instructional computers. CUSG and Sung Hwan Kim (currently representing the grad students) support the installation and usage for researchers on the research computers. The De

researchers on the research computers. The Device Group has a copy of the software on its own file server, and the Device Group sys admins can update that as needed via 'ftp ftp.synopsys.com'. BWRC may be running their own license server, as

BWRC may be running their own license server, as well. Synopsys University Program Licensing Policy: "Synopsys University Program sells Licensed Software (3355-0) to a specific Licensee/Department; persons within the same department can u

rtment; persons within the same department can use the Licensed Software as long as they have permission from Licensee/Department and they abide by the terms and conditions of the Synopsys University End-User Software License Agreement (EUL) a

sity End-User Software License Agreement (EUL) and any Supplement(s) of the EUL. If persons outside of the specified department wishes to use the Licensed Software they must purchase they own Licensed Software.' The University Bundle product

censed Software.' The University Bundle products are listed at (a password is required; ask inst@eecs or cusg@eecs to send you the contents if needed): https://www.synopsys.com/apps/protected/university/tools.html About installing licenses

university/tools.html About installing licenses: http://www.synopsys.com/support/keys/ For more information: http://www.synopsys.com/Community/UniversityProgram/ /share/b/pub/tcad.help /share/b/pub/hspice.help Synopsys Documentation an

are/b/pub/hspice.help Synopsys Documentation and on-line Help --------------------------------------- Synopsys documentation is at http://www.synopsys.com/news/pubs/ For the current products and changes from previous releases, please see ht

nd changes from previous releases, please see https://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/pub/nis/?file=Synopsys/docs/Current-Bundle.pdf (Login using an EECS Instructional UNIX account). It includes * lists of all tools * new tools are highlighte

* lists of all tools * new tools are highlighted in green * warnings that some products will be deleted after this release * Product Feature List and matching License Key Name On Instructional systems, see /share/instsww/synopsys/2003.03/do

al systems, see /share/instsww/synopsys/2003.03/doc or our password-protected site at http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/pub/ For information about Synopsys on EECS research computers, please see http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/~cxh/synopsy

ee http://ptolemy.eecs.berkeley.edu/~cxh/synopsys.html On-line training: https://www.synopsys.com/protected/partners/university/members.html SolvNet (https://solvnet.synopsys.com/) is the Synopsys on-line help resource. All instructors

ynopsys on-line help resource. All instructors and students can have access to training (limits for students) and SolvNet resource materials; they can register onto SolvNet using a University email address (berkeley.edu) and the UCB Site

y email address (berkeley.edu) and the UCB Site ID number. To get the UCB Site ID number, send a request to inst@eecs.berkeley.edu from a berkeley.edu email address. SolvNet on-line tutorials include topics in * Digital System Architectu

ls include topics in * Digital System Architecture and Design (ICs, VLSI) * Analog/RF Design * IC/Semiconductor Fabrication * Implementation * Verification (Verilog) Only selected EECS staff are allowed to post questions to Synopsys.

S staff are allowed to post questions to Synopsys. Students running Synopsys tools for a class can ask "inst@eecs.berkeley.edu". EECS Researchers running Synopsys tools can ask "help@eecs.berkeley.edu". Synopsys buys Synplicity ----------------

.edu". Synopsys buys Synplicity ------------------------ Synopsys acquired Synplicity, Inc (http://www.synplicity.com) in May 2008, including SynplifyPro (/share/b/pub/synplify.help). Sentaurus (SWB): Tecplot replaced by Svisual -------------

s (SWB): Tecplot replaced by Svisual -------------------------------------------- (Oct 2013) Tecplot is no longer licensed, and is replaced by Svisual. The introduction of Svisual ("Sentaurus Visual") changes plotting and scripting. It repl

sual") changes plotting and scripting. It replaces Tecplot and the Synopsys "tecplot_sv" launcher. Svisual is only on our Linux systems; there is no release for Solaris. The Sentaurus Visual manual on the Instructional Linux systems is in

manual on the Instructional Linux systems is in /share/instsww/synopsys/docs/svisual_ug.pdf and under https://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/pub/nis/?file=Synopsys/docs/. Sentaurus Visual is a newly developed tool that has the same functionalit

ewly developed tool that has the same functionality as Tecplot. It is more powerful than Tecplot because it supports TCL scripting. S-Visual allows you to plot simulation results in one, two and three dimensions. It supports scripts written

three dimensions. It supports scripts written in either Inspect or TCL. To start Sentaurus Visual from the command line, type: svisual To start Sentaurus Visual with help, type: svisual -h Example: svisual n2_fps.tdr The example loads t

Example: svisual n2_fps.tdr The example loads the dataset associated with a file and generates its plot. Sentaurus Visual is integrated in the Sentaurus Workbench graphical interface. You can start Sentaurus Visual by either: 1) Clicking a

start Sentaurus Visual by either: 1) Clicking a node, which displays the Node Explorer. In the Node Explorer, in the Viewer box, select svisual and click the Launch button next to it. 2) Clicking the Visualize toolbar button and selecting S

cking the Visualize toolbar button and selecting Sentaurus Visual. See the Sentaurus Visual User Guide (H-2013.03) page 163 for a list of Inspect commands are no longer supported in Sentaurus Visual. (/share/instsww/synopsys/H-2013.03/doc/svi

sual. (/share/instsww/synopsys/H-2013.03/doc/svisual_ug.pdf) Sentaurus Visual is installed on the Instructional Linux systems in /share/instsww/synopsys/H_2013.03-SP1 It will also soon be on research Linux systems in /usr/eesww/synopsys/H

research Linux systems in /usr/eesww/synopsys/H_2013.03-SP1 Synopsys desribes the migration from TechPlot to Sentaurus Visual as: "Migration is very straightforward and shouldn't cause any problems; the old tool used scripts for the plotti

oblems; the old tool used scripts for the plotting utility called Inspect. Inspect does not use OEM libraries and is still included in the new product (Sentaurus Visual). So from the scripting point of view you are well covered. The other asp

oint of view you are well covered. The other aspect of visualization is the interactive plotting of TCAD results. The new tool is easier to use and from our migration experience to date does not require more than a couple of hours to learn."

require more than a couple of hours to learn." (2012) The Sentaurus Tecplot program fails with the error X Protocol error: GLXBadContext Major opcode = 143 when it is run using the Xming window manager on a Windows computer. It see

window manager on a Windows computer. It seems to be a conflict with the graphics capability of Xming and the OpenGL graphics libraries. It can be observed with the command: /share/b/bin/swb -> Extensions -> Run Tecplot SV

/bin/swb -> Extensions -> Run Tecplot SV while you are logged into a server such as pulsar.eecs with ssh. You can run it on the SunRays in 199 Cory or 277 Soda. Unfortunately, that means you must go to the lab. Release Notes --

t means you must go to the lab. Release Notes ------------- Oct 2013: 7584-0 StarRC Ultra replaces 5337-0 StarRC (many features) start it with '/share/b/bin/StarXtract -gui' 4756-0 CustomExplorer Ultra replace 4644-0 Custom WaveView (

Explorer Ultra replace 4644-0 Custom WaveView (includes the features: sx_wva) start it with '/share/b/bin/cx' After opening cx, go to Tools > WaveView to lauch WaveView as before. 9935-0 TCAD Sentaurus Visual replaces 4349-0 TCAD Sentaurus

D Sentaurus Visual replaces 4349-0 TCAD Sentaurus WB Visualization (old features: Tecplot_all, Inspect_all) (new features: svisual_all) Tecplot is no longer licensed, and is replaced by Svisual. May 2008: Synopsys has discontinued T

May 2008: Synopsys has discontinued TCAD Sentaurus from Windows as of May 2008. Due to the decline in the demand of the Windows platform for TCAD Sentaurus, the Synopsys TCAD Sentaurus products release A-2007.12, was the final sh

us products release A-2007.12, was the final shipping release for this platform. Subsequent new releases will no longer support the Microsoft Windows platform. (http://www.synopsys.com/products/platforms/notices/microsoft_may08.html) Dec

ts/platforms/notices/microsoft_may08.html) Dec 2005: Synopsys will no longer offer FPGA Compiler II in the University Program Tool Package; there is no replacement product. Synopsys will continue to provide support to universities with exi
nue to provide support to universities with existing FPGA Compiler II licenses through the duration of the license period. Instructional Support Group 378/386 Cory, 333 Soda inst@eecs.berkeley.edu
